'''Danny Moore''' (born 2 November 1971) is an [[Australians|Australian]] former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. An [[Australian Kangaroos|Australian international]] and [[Queensland Maroons|Queensland]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative who played most of his career either at {{rlp|CE}} or on the {{rlp|WG}}, he played his club football in Australia for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] (with whom he won the [[1996 ARL premiership]]) and the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. He also played in England for the [[Wigan Warriors]] (with whom he won 1998's [[Super League III]]) and the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]].1st player to win a grand final in the Northern and Southern Hemisphere

==Playing career==
{{BLP unreferenced section|date=May 2017}}

===1990s===
A Townsville junior, Moore's early career was played in the [[Rugby league positions#The back row|back row]]. He joined the Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles as a {{rlp|LK}} forward where he made his first grade début in the round 7 clash of the [[1991 NSWRL season]] against the [[Parramatta Eels]] at [[Brookvale Oval]], playing from the bench in Manly's 30–18 win. After spending the rest of the 1991 and all of [[1992 NSWRL season|1992]] playing in [[NSW Cup|Reserve Grade]], he was switched to the centres at the start of the [[1993 NSWRFL season]] and became a regular first grader.

Moore moved to the [[Super League]] to play for English club [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 1998, appearing at centre in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

===2000s===
Moore shifted to the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] for 2000's [[Super League V]]. His final year was the [[2001 NRL season]] in which he played two games for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].
